system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

{

    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;

    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;

}

}

{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

We continue to see the same cascade:
1. **`log.blockMesh`**: Block 1 (`hex (8 12 13 9 10 14 15 11)`) has "inward-pointing faces": `4(11 15 14 10)`
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find mesh files (cascading from blockMesh failure)

## Root Cause — The Bend Block Has Inverted Faces

Looking at Block 1: `hex (8 12 13 9 10 14 15 11)`

Vertices:
- 8: (0, 25, -25), 12: (25, -125, -25), 13: (25, -125, 25), 9: (0, 25, 25)
- 10: (25, -125, -25), 14: (25, -75, 25), 15: (25, -75, -25), 11: (0, 25, -25)

The first face is `8 12 13 9` = `(0,25,-25)-(25,-125,-25)-(25,-125,25)-(0,25,25)`. Viewed from outside the block (i.e., in the direction of the normal pointing outward), this face goes from (0,25) down to (25,-125) then back up to (0,25) — tracing the path: right, up, left. This is **clockwise** when viewed from above, meaning the normal points inward instead of outward.

The face ordering needs to be reversed so that when viewed from outside the block, the vertices go counter-clockwise.

## Recommended Fix

Change the vertex ordering in **Block 1** from:
```
hex (8 12 13 9 10 14 15 11)
```
to:
```
hex (8 9 13 12 10 11 15 14)
```

This reverses the winding of the first face: `8 9 13 12` = `(0,25,-25)-(0,25,25)-(25,-125,25)-(25,-125,-25)`. Now viewed from above, this goes: up, right, down — which is **counter-clockwise**, giving an outward-pointing normal.

Similarly, verify and fix the orderings in **Block 0** (`hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)`) and **Block 2** (`hex (16 17 21 18 20 23 22 19)`) if needed. The standard convention for a hex block `hex(v0 v1 v2 v3 v4 v5 v6 v7)` is:
- Face 0-1-2-3: bottom face, CCW when viewed from below (normal in -z)
- Face 4-5-6-7: top face, CCW when viewed from above (normal in +z)
- Side faces follow accordingly

For Block 0: `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` with vertices defining a block from x=-50..0, y=-25..25, z=-25..25, this should already be correct. For Block 2: `hex (16 17 21 18 20 23 22 19)` with vertices at x=-500..-50, y=-125..-75, z=-25..25, the face `16 17 21 18` goes from (-500,-125,-25)→(-500,-75,-25)→(-50,-75,-25)→(-50,-125,-25) — traced right, up, left — clockwise. Reverse it to `hex (16 18 21 17 20 22 23 18)` or equivalently `hex (16 18 21 17 20 22 23 18)` — actually the correct reversal is `hex (16 18 17 21 20 22 19 23)` to maintain consistent opposite-face pairing.

## Summary of Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `system/blockMeshDict` | Reverse the vertex ordering in Block 1 from `hex (8 12 13 9 10 14 15 11)` to `hex (8 9 13 12 10 11 15 14)`; similarly fix Block 2 if needed to ensure all faces follow the right-hand rule |
| All other files | No changes needed |</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
